vision: Ensi is an ACP-compatible agent that brings structured software planning (BMAD Analyst → PM → Architect) into the Multica workspace as autonomous teammates, eliminating the gap between ideation and implementation.
problem_statement: Teams on Multica skip structured planning and jump straight to coding agents, causing rework, scope creep, and missed requirements. BMad Method (45k+ stars) provides the methodology but is CLI-only and stateless. Multica has the platform but no planning layer. Ensi bridges them via ACP.

personas.name: Marco
personas.profile: Full-stack dev, uses Multica to delegate coding tasks to agents. Comfortable with AI tooling.
pain_points: Starts coding before the problem is fully understood
Wastes cycles on features nobody needs
goals: Get a structured brief and feature list before assigning coding issues — without leaving Multica
personas.trigger: Creates an issue tagged BP (Brainstorm/Planning); Ensi runs automatically

personas.name: Ana
personas.profile: Leads a small product team of 3–5 devs. Uses Multica as the team's project board.
goals: Ensi acts as a planning teammate — produces briefs and ADRs the team can review before any code is written
personas.trigger: Tags issues for planning; uses artefacts as input to sprint planning

personas.name: James
personas.profile: Uses BMad via Claude Code or Cursor. Values structured planning but dislikes CLI-only workflows.
pain_points: Each BMad session is ephemeral — no state persistence
No team visibility, no audit trail
goals: Resume sessions, share artefacts with teammates, see progress on a board
personas.trigger: Discovery of Ensi brings him to Multica; familiar with the BMad persona names

key_goals: Deliver BMAD Analyst → PM → Architect pipeline inside Multica as a single ACP agent
Persist all artefacts (product-brief.md, prd.md, architecture.md) in .ensi/docs/ within the project repo
Stateful session management — sessions can be paused and resumed; state survives process restarts
Handoff protocol — each stage produces a structured output and triggers the next stage automatically
Zero-config onboarding — developers add Ensi as a Multica agent with no BMad CLI installation required
non_goals_v1: Developer/coding agent — Ensi does planning only
Custom LLM provider support — MVP uses Anthropic API only
Existing-codebase analysis — MVP scope is greenfield projects only
UI customisation — artefacts are plain markdown
Billing / metering — no per-token accounting in MVP

should.name: Human approval gate between pipeline stages
Stage completion notifications via Multica comments
Exponential backoff + retry on LLM errors

could.name: Automated sprint planning from PRD (Scrum Master agent)

competitive_landscape.name: GitHub Copilot Workspace
competitive_landscape.approach: Plan → approve → execute; strong GitHub Issues integration
competitive_landscape.gap: Proprietary, GitHub-only, no persistent BMAD-style artefacts

competitive_landscape.name: Devin 2.0
competitive_landscape.gap: No structured planning methodology; not designed for planning-first workflows

competitive_landscape.name: BMad Method (raw)
competitive_landscape.gap: CLI-only, IDE-dependent, stateless, no platform integration

competitive_landscape.name: CrewAI
competitive_landscape.gap: Framework only — no PM layer, no artifact validation, no pipeline FSM

competitive_landscape.name: Chat-only AI (Claude, GPT)
competitive_landscape.gap: No structure, no persistence, no schema validation, no team visibility

technical: Must be ACP-compatible (Agent Client Protocol, Apache 2.0, TypeScript SDK)
Must integrate with Multica daemon/CLI architecture
Artefacts stored in .ensi/docs/ within the checked-out project repo
LLM: Anthropic API (Claude Sonnet/Haiku); MVP does not support other providers
Runtime: Node.js v20+ (TypeScript/Node.js, consistent with ACP SDK)
Session state: must survive process restarts (file-based or embedded DB)
business: MVP scope: 3 agents (Analyst, PM, Architect), 1 workflow (greenfield)
No external service dependencies beyond Anthropic API and Multica daemon
compliance: No PII stored in artefacts beyond what the user explicitly inputs
API keys must never be persisted to disk in plaintext; use environment variables
constraints.timeline: No hard deadline; MVP deliverable in a single sprint (1–2 weeks)

risks.title: ACP SDK immaturity — breaking changes or missing features
risks.mitigation: Pin SDK version; abstract behind thin adapter layer

risks.title: Multica daemon API changes break agent registration
risks.mitigation: Use only stable CLI commands; monitor changelog; integration tests

risks.title: LLM output non-determinism breaks structured handoffs
risks.mitigation: Define strict output schemas per stage; validate before advancing; retry on parse failure

risks.title: BMad workflow doesn't translate to async Multica task model
risks.mitigation: Prototype Analyst stage as a spike before building PM/Architect stages

risks.title: Anthropic API latency/cost makes sessions feel slow or expensive
risks.mitigation: Use Haiku for iterative steps; Sonnet for final artefact generation

assumptions_to_validate.statement: ACP TypeScript SDK agents can register with the Multica daemon without custom fork
assumptions_to_validate.validation: Run a minimal ACP hello-world agent against the daemon before scoping the full build

assumptions_to_validate.statement: BMad prompts produce useful output via Anthropic API without IDE context
assumptions_to_validate.validation: Test BMad prompts directly via API with a sample greenfield project

assumptions_to_validate.statement: File-based state (.ensi/state.json) is sufficient for MVP session resumption
assumptions_to_validate.validation: Model the state schema explicitly; prototype resume flow

assumptions_to_validate.statement: Issue tag trigger (BP) is the right UX for Multica users
assumptions_to_validate.validation: User interviews or Discord feedback before investing in alternative triggers

assumptions_to_validate.statement: Three agents (Analyst, PM, Architect) cover 80% of greenfield planning needs

recommendations_to_pm: Finalize artifact schemas BEFORE writing persona prompts
Reduce pipeline MVP to 5 stages (defer Deploy + Observability to v2)
Ship only Code Reviewer as the sole transversal agent in MVP
Both validation spikes (ACP integration + BMad prompt portability) must pass before feature development begins
